channel.title: Cadence 步频 — PT Research Signal
channel.description: Daily curated physical therapy & rehab research for clinicians. AI-scored signal from 50+ journals.

item.title: Critically appraised papers: Adding physiotherapy or motivational text messages does not improve pain beyond home exercise alone for degenerative meniscal tear and knee pain [commentary].
item.description: This commentary on a critically appraised paper suggests that for degenerative meniscal tears and knee pain, adding physiotherapy or motivational text messages to home exercise does not improve pain outcomes.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Critically appraised paper: Mobile-based pulmonary rehabilitation improved exercise capacity as much as and health status more than centre-based pulmonary rehabilitation for chronic obstructive pulmonary disease [synopsis].
item.description: This critically appraised paper indicates that mobile-based pulmonary rehabilitation improved exercise capacity as much as, and health status more than, center-based pulmonary rehabilitation for chronic obstructive pulmonary disease patients.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Smallest worthwhile effect of anterior cruciate ligament reconstruction compared with non-surgical management: a benefit-harm trade-off study.
item.description: A benefit-harm trade-off study quantified the smallest worthwhile effect of ACL reconstruction versus non-surgical management, as perceived by 92 recreational or competitive athletes, for improving knee function and return to sport.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Recent highlights in motor recovery and rehabilitation research after stroke.
item.description: A systematic review identified 118 RCTs, protocols, and systematic reviews with meta-analyses since 2020, highlighting recent advances in motor recovery and rehabilitation research after stroke and providing recommendations for future trials.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Multi-Domain Benefits of Exergaming for Sarcopenia Management in Institutionalized Older Adults: Physical, Psychological, and Social Outcomes
item.description: Exergaming offers multi-domain benefits for sarcopenia management in institutionalized older adults, encompassing physical, psychological, and social outcomes, aligning with a biopsychosocial understanding of the condition.
item.author: Archives of PM&R

item.title: Innovative technologies for gait rehabilitation in incomplete spinal cord injury: A systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis of studies from inception to March 2025 found innovative technological interventions effectively improve gait-related outcomes in adults with incomplete spinal cord injury.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Two-Year Outcomes of the Proximal Hamstring Avulsion Rehabilitation Regimes: Longitudinal Versus Accelerated Protocol (PHARRLAP) Study: A Randomized Controlled Trial.
item.description: A prospective RCT (n=60) with 2-year follow-up found no significant differences in clinical outcomes, strength, or functional symmetry between conservative and accelerated rehabilitation protocols after proximal hamstring tendon repair.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Read more about Calendar Year 2027 Hospital Outpatient Prospective Payment System (OPPS) and Ambulatory Surgical Center (ASC) Proposed Rule (CMS-1850-P)
item.description: The CMS has issued its proposed rule for the Calendar Year 2027 Hospital Outpatient Prospective Payment System (OPPS) and Ambulatory Surgical Center (ASC), which will dictate payment policies for these settings.
item.author: CMS

item.title: Establishing weighted criteria for return to play after hamstring injury: a systematic review and methodological quality analysis.
item.description: A systematic review identified weighted criteria for return to play after hamstring injury, highlighting a lack of clear consensus despite high recurrence rates.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Shortwave diathermy for managing knee osteoarthritis: A systematic review with meta-analysis.
item.description: A systematic review with meta-analysis of 17 trials (n=1,372) found that shortwave diathermy significantly improved pain (mean -2.13; 95% CI [-2.64, -1.62]; p < .01) and walking ability (mean -0.72; 95% CI [not reported]) in knee osteoarthritis.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Read more about CMS Proposes Updates to Strengthen Medicare Program Integrity, Combat Fraud, and Expand Access to Home Health Care
item.description: CMS proposes updates to strengthen Medicare program integrity, combat fraud, and expand access to home health care in the US.
item.author: CMS

item.title: Patterns of Outcome Measure Utilization in Stroke Rehabilitation Lower Extremity Trials Conducted in Low-to-Middle versus High-Income Countries.
item.description: A systematic review of 1,548 RCTs found that low-to-middle income countries utilized more generalized impairment and activities of daily living outcome measures in stroke rehabilitation lower extremity trials compared to high-income countries, often relying on patient observation.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Anti-Compensatory Saccades Changes After Visuo-Vestibular Physical Therapy in People With Acute Unilateral Vestibulopathy: A Prospective Observational Study.
item.description: In 92 patients with acute unilateral vestibulopathy, an 8-week visuo-vestibular physical therapy program improved anti-compensatory saccades, which reflect vestibulo-saccadic interaction.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Prioritising fall prevention strategies in Parkinson's disease: A multi-method evaluation.
item.description: A multi-method study integrating a structured narrative review, Delphi process, and Best-Worst Method quantitatively prioritized fall prevention strategies for Parkinson's disease.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Associations of neuropathic pain and pain catastrophizing with rehabilitation outcomes in patients with stroke.
item.description: This prospective observational study found that neuropathic pain and pain catastrophizing are common after stroke and are associated with poorer rehabilitation outcomes, impacting functional status and quality of life after a 4-week rehabilitation program.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Comparative Effects of Land-Based and Aquatic Physiotherapy on Functional and Neuromuscular Outcomes in the Rigid-Akinetic Subtype of Parkinson's Disease: A Randomized Clinical Trial.
item.description: This randomized clinical trial involving 30 individuals with rigid-akinetic Parkinson's disease found that both 12 weeks of land-based and aquatic physiotherapy improved motor function, providing comparative effects on functional and neuromuscular outcomes.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Health Literacy During Inpatient Rehabilitation and Its Association with One-Year Health and Functional Outcomes in Individuals with Traumatic Spinal Cord Injury.
item.description: A cohort study (N=3,943) found that limited health literacy during inpatient rehabilitation for traumatic spinal cord injury was associated with poorer one-year health and functional outcomes, including higher rates of UTIs, pressure injuries, and rehospitalizations.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Relationship Between Sarcopenia and Intensity-Specific Physical Activity in Older Adults Living in a Rural Area: An Isotemporal Substitution Analysis from the NEIGE Study.
item.description: This study found that in community-dwelling older adults (n=527), replacing sedentary behavior with moderate-to-vigorous physical activity was associated with a lower risk of sarcopenia, using an isotemporal substitution model.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Preoperative physiotherapy and one-year patient-reported outcomes after primary total knee arthroplasty: a registry-based cohort study of 1,688 patients.
item.description: A registry-based cohort study of 1,688 patients undergoing primary total knee arthroplasty found no significant association between preoperative physiotherapy and improved patient-reported outcomes or pain at 2 months and 1 year postoperatively.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The Perspective and Needs of Older Adults With Osteoporosis With a Recent Upper Extremity Fragility Fracture Concerning Fall Risk and Fear of Falling.
item.description: This qualitative study explored the perspectives of older adults with osteoporosis who had upper extremity fractures, focusing on their health concerns, the impact of falls and fear of falling (FoF) on daily life, and their views on rehabilitation.
item.author: PubMed
